paper_title: Elastic Weight Consolidation Improves the Robustness of Self-Supervised Learning Methods under Transfer

methodology.conceptual_framework: Treat SSL pre-training and fine-tuning as sequential tasks

datasets.name: ImageNet1k
datasets.usage: Pre-training with subset for FIM computation

datasets.name: CIFAR10
datasets.usage: Validation of FIM accuracy via reverse transfer

datasets.name: Waterbirds
datasets.usage: Evaluation for worst group accuracy improvement

datasets.name: Celeb-A
datasets.usage: Evaluation for worst group accuracy improvement

key_findings.observation: FIM for ViT-B/16 is poorly conditioned
key_findings.impact: Makes it impossible to fully recover SSL performance on ImageNet1k

key_findings.result: Regularization improves worst group accuracy on Waterbirds by 5%
Regularization improves worst group accuracy on Celeb-A by 2%

problem_addressed: Drift in self-supervised learning representations during fine-tuning to downstream tasks, and catastrophic forgetting in sequential learning
title: Elastic Weight Consolidation Improves the Robustness of Self-Supervised Learning Methods under Transfer
